blocks:
Ayahs

سَبِّحِ ٱسْمَ رَبِّكَ ٱلْأَعْلَى (۱)
Celebrate the Name of your Lord, the Most Exalted, (1)
ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ فَسَوَّىٰ (۲)
who created and proportioned, (2)
وَٱلَّذِى قَدَّرَ فَهَدَىٰ (۳)
who determined and guided, (3)
وَٱلَّذِىٓ أَخْرَجَ ٱلْمَرْعَىٰ (٤)
who brought forth the pasture (4)
فَجَعَلَهُۥ غُثَآءً أَحْوَىٰ (٥)
and then turned it into a black scum. (5)

Key Points

Glorifying the Name of Allah means not only that we should not associate partners with the Essence (Dhat) of Allah, but also that we should not associate partners even with His Name, nor place the names of others on the same level as the Name of Allah. Just as some syncretic groups used to say: "In the Name of God and in the Name of the heroic people of Iran." There are many signs in the Quran and Hadith indicating that Islam pays attention to respecting sacred names. As He says in this Surah: besides glorifying the Essence, you must also purify the Name of your Lord. Elsewhere He says: Do not address the Prophet by his name casually, like you address one another: «لاتجعلوا دعاء الرسول كدعاء بعضكم بعضا»(2) Elsewhere He says: Why do they name the angels with female names: «لیسمّون الملائكة تسمیة الانثى»(3) And the Prophet of Islam used to change the inappropriate names of his companions.

The issue of creation and guidance is also mentioned in Ayah 50 of Surah Taha, where Pharaoh asked Prophet Moses عليه السلام: Who is your Lord? He replied: «ربّنا الّذى اعطى كلّ شى ء خلقه ثم هَدى» (Our Lord is He Who gave everything its creation, then guided it.) It is obvious that the intent of this guidance is the inherent (takwini) guidance that Allah has placed within creatures. For example, He guided the structure of the mother's body to produce milk, and He guided the infant to seek the mother's breast and suckle milk. The inherent guidance of creatures is better understood over time. This is the very matter that has driven thousands of scientists to research how birds or fish return to their original location even if they are hundreds of kilometers away. Divine guidance has placed something in every creature that advances it along its path of perfection. Particles of dust become fruit, fruit becomes sperm, and then a human, and again the human body returns to dust.

«سَوّى» (Sawwa) means to establish order. This ranges from the order of the galaxies to the order governing a microscopic insect. «غُثاء» (Ghutha) means dry vegetation and debris, and «أحوى » (Ahwa) means greenery that tends toward blackness. Even a small leaf and insignificant debris are not left to their own devices. The greenery that appears dry and dark to us is within the system of Divine guidance, because those same dried leaves turn into another substance necessary for the strength and nourishment of the earth and the tree, or under massive pressure, they turn into coal, or have other beneficial effects in the soil.

2) Nur, 63. 3) Najm, 27.

Messages

  1. The Name of Allah is sacred. Avoid any disrespect toward His Name. «سبّح اسم ربّك»
  2. Do not place unworthy attributes alongside the Name of Allah. «سبّح اسم ربّك»
  3. Do not bestow the exclusive Names of Allah upon others. «سبح اسم ربّك»
  4. Glorifying Him is the cause of your growth and nurturing. «سبح اسم ربّك»
  5. The responsibility of eliminating polytheism rests primarily upon the heavenly leader and subsequently upon all believers. «سبّح»
  6. Perfect Monotheism (Tawhid) is achieved through the purification of His Essence (Tanzih al-Dhat) and the glorification of His Name (Tasbih al-Ism). «سبّح اسم ربّك»
  7. Allah is superior to everything that is said, written, or imagined. He is the Most High in all perfections. «ربّك الاعلى»
  8. Allah is not only the Creator, but He is also the Organizer of existence and creatures. «خلق فَسَوّى»
  9. The capacity and guidance of creatures are proof of the purposeful nature of creation. «قدّر فَهدى»
  10. Divine commands are accompanied by wisdom and reason. Glorify the Name of Allah, because He is the Most High, He created and ordered, and He guided. «سبّح... الاعلى، خلق فسوّى و الّذى قدّر فهدى»
  11. The inherent guidance of creation appears in various forms, sometimes green and sometimes dry, but both are within the orbit of guidance. «اخرج المرعى فجعله غثاء احوى»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-A'la

This Surah has nineteen verses and was revealed in Mecca. The Surahs that begin with the command to glorify Allah are called the Musabbihat (Glorifiers), and Surah Al-A'la is the last of the Musabbihat Surahs. The name of the Surah is derived from the first verse, which describes Allah with the attribute of «اعلى» (Al-A'la, The Most High). The contents of the Surah are divided into two sections: the first section addresses the noble Prophet of Islam, and the second section concerns the believers and the disbelievers, and the factors leading to the felicity and wretchedness of these two groups. It has been recommended in narrations that Surah Al-A'la should be recited in the first unit (rak'ah) of the Eid prayers (Fitr and Qurban).(1)

1) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

سَنُقْرِئُكَ فَلَا تَنسَىٰٓ (٦)
We shall have you recite [the Quran], then you will not forget [any of it] (6)
إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ يَعْلَمُ ٱلْجَهْرَ وَمَا يَخْفَىٰ (۷)
except what Allah may wish. Indeed, He knows the open and what is hidden. (7)

Key Points

«سنقرئك» is derived from the root «قرء» (Qara’a) and the If’āl conjugation form. Linguistically, Iqrā’ means that the reciter reads while the master listens and corrects his mistakes. However, regarding the Noble Prophet of Islam, Iqrā’ means: We grant you the power to recite the Quran correctly, without any omission or deficiency, and that you will not forget what has been revealed to you. (4) Following «سنقرئك فلا تنسى», the phrase «الا ما شاء اللّه» is an exception included to express the absolute Divine power, making it clear that Allah is capable of causing the Prophet to forget, even after granting him this power. (Just as He says concerning the inhabitants of Paradise: Paradise is eternal for them, and Allah’s bounty toward them will not be cut off or severed, yet between the words «خالدین» (eternal) and «عطاء غیر مجذوذ» (unfailing bounty), He still says: «الاّ ما شاء ربّك» (5) — unless your Lord wills [otherwise], meaning He could expel them from Paradise and cut off His grace. This is stated to show that the Hand of God is not constrained.)

4) Tafsir al-Mizan. 5) Hud, 108.

Messages

1- The creation of existence and its inherent (cosmic) guidance is a prelude to the legislative and spiritual guidance of humanity. «سنقرئك» (How could the God Who draws the plant out from within the soil fail to manifest the spiritual potentials of man? «سنقرئك»)

2- The Prophet, whose entire being relies on revelation, was concerned about forgetting it in the future, so Allah promised him that he would not forget. «سنقرئك فلا تَنسى»

3- The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله is immune from all forms of forgetfulness (Nisyān). «فَلا تَنسى»

4- The Prophet was the preserver (Hāfiz) of the entire Quran. «فَلا تنسى» (One of the miracles of the Noble Prophet was that upon hearing something once, he remembered it forever, even lengthy verses.)

5- The Hand of God is not constrained, and He can take back what He gives. «سنقرئك فلا تنسى الاّ ما شاء اللّه»

6- Although Allah never breaks His promise «ولن یخلف اللّه وعده» (6), the meaning of Divine promises is not that Allah is compelled. «فلا تَنسى الاّ ما شاء اللّه»

7- The Divine Will is wise and knowledgeable. «الاّ ما شاء اللّه انّه یعلم...»

8- The nature of some matters requires them to be hidden. «ما یخفى» (The word «یخفى» in the form of the present tense verb indicates that the concealment of some matters is necessitated by their very nature.) 6) Hajj, 47.

Ayahs

وَنُيَسِّرُكَ لِلْيُسْرَىٰ (۸)
And We shall ease you into facility. (8)
فَذَكِّرْ إِن نَّفَعَتِ ٱلذِّكْرَىٰ (۹)
So admonish, for admonition is indeed beneficial: (9)
سَيَذَّكَّرُ مَن يَخْشَىٰ (۱٠)
he who fears [God] will take admonition, (10)
وَيَتَجَنَّبُهَا ٱلْأَشْقَى (۱۱)
and the most wretched will shun it (11)
ٱلَّذِى يَصْلَى ٱلنَّارَ ٱلْكُبْرَىٰ (۱۲)
—he who will enter the Great Fire, (12)
ثُمَّ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحْيَىٰ (۱۳)
then he will neither live in it, nor die. (13)

Key Points

Prophet Moses, for success in carrying out his mission (propagation), asked Allah for two things: first, breadth of chest (patience/tolerance), and second, the easing of the matter of his apostleship: «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى و یسّر لى امرى» (7) Allah granted these two matters to the Prophet of Islam without him even asking. Just as regarding the breadth of chest, He said: «ألم نشرح لك صدرك» (8) and regarding the easing of the matter of the call (Da'wah), He says in this Ayah: «و نیسّرك للیسرى» (9). The meaning of «نیسّرك» is that, O Prophet! We prepare your noble soul with great character, breadth of chest, purity, and simplicity, such that He can carry out the duty of apostleship easily and in the best manner. We make it so that you yourself choose the easy path, which is the path of innate nature (Fitrah). (10)

Conditions for reminding and admonition (Maw'izah): A: The condition for admonition is the readiness of the admonisher. «نیسّرك... فذكّر» B: Reminding and admonition must be based on the Quran. «سنقرئك... فذكّر» C: The admonisher must not himself be prone to heedlessness and forgetfulness. «فلا تنسى... فذكّر» D: A successful admonisher is one who is hopeful of divine assistance. «سنقرئك... نیسّرك... فذكّر»

7) Taha, 25 - 26. 8) Sharh, 1. 9) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 10) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Propagating the religion and inviting people toward Allah involves many difficulties and hardships, but Allah has promised to assist the propagator of the religion and make the endurance of hardships easy for him. «نیسرك للیسرى»

2- To whomever you assign a mission, remove the knots (difficulties) from his task. «نیسرك للیسرى فذكّر»

3- Our glorification (Tasbih) is a prelude to Allah's easing of affairs. «سبّح اسم ربّك ... نیسرك للیُسرى»

4- The revelation of the Wahy (divine inspiration) is for the purpose of reminding people. «سنقرئك... فذكّر»

5- The condition for reminding is the acceptance of advice, and the Prophet has no responsibility toward those who refuse advice. «فذكّر اِن نفعت الذكرى»

6- Only God-fearing individuals accept the reminder. «سیذكّر من یخشى»

7- Admonishing God-fearing individuals is easier. «نیسرك للیُسرى... سیذكر من یخشى»

8- The propagator must be reassured that his work is not in vain. «فذكّر... سیذكّر...»

9- The propagator should not expect immediate effect. «سیذكّر»

10- The problem of the disbelievers is their own distance from the Quran and the Prophet, not that there is any weakness or defect in these two pure elements. «یتجنّبها»

11- The Quran is the standard (Mizan). The one who accepts its advice receives the medal of «یَخشى» (fearing God): «سیذكر من یخشى», and the one who rejects its advice receives the title of «اشقى» (most wretched): «و یتجنّبها الاشقى».

12- Distance from the advice and message of the Quran leads to wretchedness (Shaqawah). «یتجنّبها الاشقى»

13- The permanent characteristic of the wretched is avoidance of advice. «یتجنبها» is in the present tense form to convey the meaning of continuity and permanence.

14- The most wretched person is the one who lacks the fear of Allah (Khashyat Ilahi). «اَشقى» is placed opposite «یَخشى».

15- Do not expect the reformation of all people. «سیذكر من یخشى و یتجنّبها الاشقى»

16- The factor for acceptance or non-acceptance lies within the human being himself. «یخشى... اشقى»

17- Punishment is commensurate with wickedness. «اشقى - النار الكبرى»

18- Hellfire is eternal and everlasting for a group of people. «لایموت فیها و لایحیى»

19- Eternity (Khulud) in Hellfire does not change the nature of the inhabitants of Hell such that they no longer suffer from the fire; rather, they are constantly in torment. «لایموت فیها و لا یحیى»

Ayahs

قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ (۱٤)
‘Felicitous is he who purifies himself, (14)
وَذَكَرَ ٱسْمَ رَبِّهِۦ فَصَلَّىٰ (۱٥)
celebrates the Name of his Lord, and prays. (15)

Key Points

Tazkiyah (purification) is that the intellect is cleansed of corrupt beliefs, the soul is cleansed of corrupt morals, and the limbs are cleansed of corrupt behavior. Zakat (alms/purification) is the cleansing of the spirit from greed and stinginess, and the cleansing of wealth from the rights of the deprived. In the school of the Prophets, the successful ones are the pure ones: «قد افلح من تزكى» (He who purifies himself has succeeded). But in the school of the tyrants (Taghut), the successful ones are the powerful ones. Pharaoh used to say: Whoever prevails today is successful. «قد افلح الیوم من استعلى»(11) In the Quran, definite success is mentioned both for the believers: «قد افلح المؤمنون»(12) (The believers have certainly succeeded), and for those who have purified themselves: «قد افلح من تزكّى». Therefore, the true believers are those who have purified themselves.

(11) Taha, 65. (12) Al-Mu'minun, 1.

Messages

1- At the end of some verses, the Quran states: It is hoped that you will succeed. «لعلّكم تفلحون» (so that you may succeed). This is because good deeds performed by individuals who have not purified themselves are erased by hypocrisy (riya'), arrogance, sin, and the act of reminding others of favors (mann). But concerning those who are purified, He states: They are certainly successful. «قد افلح من تزكى»

2- Purification (Tazkiyah) does not happen automatically; one must actively seek it. «تزكىّ»

3- Purification (Tazkiyah) is not achieved without prayer (Salat) and worship. «تزكّى، ذكر اسم ربّه فصلّى»

Ayahs

بَلْ تُؤْثِرُونَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا (۱٦)
But you prefer the life of this world, (16)
وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ (۱۷)
while the Hereafter is better and more lasting.’ (17)
إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَفِى ٱلصُّحُفِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ (۱۸)
This is indeed in the former scriptures, (18)
صُحُفِ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَمُوسَىٰ (۱۹)
the scriptures of Abraham and Moses. (19)

Key Points

In narrations, the love of the world is the source of all errors. «حبّ الدنیا رأس كل خطیئة»(13) The world is a bridge for passage, not a stopping place; one must cross it to reach the dwelling place. «خذوا من ممرّكم لمقرّكم»(14) In the Quran, Zakat usually follows prayer: «یقیمون الصّلاة و یؤتون الزكاة»(15) But Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says that on Eid al-Fitr, paying Zakat to the poor comes before the Eid prayer. He then recited this Ayah:(16) «قد افلح من تزكّى و ذكر اسم ربّه فصلّى» Imam Ridha عليه السلام was asked about the meaning of the Ayah: «ذكر اسم ربّه فَصَلى». A person said: Whenever he mentions the name of God, he should pray. The Imam said: This is a difficult obligation, that a person should pray whenever the name of God is mentioned throughout the day and night. He asked: So what is the meaning? The Imam said: Whenever the name of God is mentioned, he should send blessings (Salawat) upon Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad. «كلمّا ذكر ربّه صَلّى على محمّد و اله»(17) The Hereafter is better than the world because it possesses the following characteristics: 1. Temporal eternity: «خالدین فیها»(18) 2. Spatial vastness: «عرضها كعرض السماء و الارض»(19) 3. Variety in food: «انهار من عسل»(20), «فاكهة كثیر»(21) 4. Freedom from fear and sorrow: «آمنین»(22) 5. Loyal friends: «اخواناً على سرر متقابلین»(23) 6. Beautiful spouses: «كامثال اللؤلؤ المكنون»(24) Abu Dharr narrates from the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that Allah sent one hundred and twenty-four thousand prophets, 313 of whom were Messengers (Rasul) and the rest were Prophets (Nabi), and they collectively possessed 104 books.(25)

13) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 130. 14) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 203. 15) Al-Ma'idah, 55. 16) Tahdhib al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 108. 17) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 18) Al Imran, 15. 19) Al-Hadid, 21. 20) Muhammad, 15. 21) Al-Zukhruf, 73. 22) Al-Hijr, 46. 23) Al-Hijr, 47. 24) Al-Waqi'ah, 23. 25) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The obstacle to purification (Tazkiyah) is the love of the world. «بل تؤثرون»

2- The world is not inherently bad; what is criticized is preferring it over the Hereafter. «تؤثرون»

3- Whenever you criticize something, show the good alternative and the solution. «تؤثرون... و الاخرة خیر»

4- The way to purify the human heart and mind is the constant and continuous remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah’s name. «تزكّى و ذكر اسم ربّه»

5- Attention to the manifestations of Lordship (Rububiyyah) is a factor in remembering God. «ذكر اسم ربّه»

6- Prayer must be based on remembrance and attention, not heedlessness or habit. «ذكر اسم ربّه فَصلّى»

7- Those who pray are successful when they are people of purification (Tazkiyah) and remembrance of God. «تزكّى و ذكر اسم ربّه فصَلّى»

8- Heavenly Books are consistent and harmonious with one another. «انّ هذا لفى الصحف الاولى»

9- Among the Heavenly Books, the scriptures of Prophet Abraham and Prophet Moses have a special distinction. «صحف ابراهیم و موسى»
